provider-response.rule: ProviderChatResponse must remain { message, stopReason, usage? } — the usage field is optional so claudecli and unknown providers return undefined without breaking the interface

hook-payload-optional.rule: Fields added to PostModelResponsePayload must be optional (?) — plugins receiving the event must handle undefined values to remain compatible with claudecli and failed provider calls

plugin-interface.rule: A Plugin implementation must expose { name: string; install(hooks: IHookRegistry): void | Promise<void> }. Plugin.install must not throw — errors must be caught and logged internally.

drizzle-migration.rule: New tables must be defined in schema.ts and the migration file generated via 'npm run db:generate' (drizzle-kit generate). Never write migration SQL by hand.

server-tool-pattern.rule: Each MCP tool requires four registrations in server.ts: (1) ListTools entry, (2) CallTool case, (3) inProcessDescriptors entry, (4) inProcessHandler case. Input validated with toMcpInputSchema(schema); handler wrapped with toMcpContent/toMcpErrorContent.
